# EmitHQ — Complete Documentation

> Open-source webhook infrastructure for SaaS teams. Send and receive webhooks with Standard Webhooks signing, automatic retries, and a real-time dashboard. AGPL-3.0 server + MIT SDKs. From $49/mo — filling the gap between free tiers and $490+ enterprise platforms.

## Company

Website: https://emithq.com. GitHub: https://github.com/Not-Another-Ai-Co/EmitHQ. License: AGPL-3.0 (server), MIT (SDKs). Contact: support@emithq.com.

## What EmitHQ Does

EmitHQ handles both outbound (sending webhooks to your customers' endpoints) and inbound (receiving webhooks from providers like Stripe/GitHub). Your app says "invoice.paid happened" → EmitHQ reliably delivers that to every registered URL, retries on failure, signs with HMAC-SHA256, and lets you monitor delivery status.

### Core Features

- **Outbound Delivery**: Send webhooks to customer endpoints with automatic retries, exponential backoff + jitter, and circuit breakers
- **Inbound Reception**: Receive webhooks from Stripe, GitHub, Shopify, Slack with provider-specific signature verification
- **Smart Retries**: Configurable per endpoint, up to 50 attempts with exponential backoff + jitter. Non-retriable: 400, 401, 403, 404, 410
- **Standard Webhooks Signing**: HMAC-SHA256 per the Standard Webhooks spec. Per-endpoint signing secrets (whsec_ prefix)
- **Real-Time Dashboard**: Monitor deliveries, inspect payloads, track endpoint health, replay failed events
- **Payload Transformation**: JSONPath extraction + template expressions. No code required. Available on Starter+ plans ($49/mo)
- **Multi-Tenant Security**: PostgreSQL Row-Level Security for tenant isolation. Per-endpoint signing secrets. API keys with zero-downtime rotation
- **Dead Letter Queue**: After all retries exhausted, messages move to DLQ for manual replay
- **Circuit Breaker**: Per endpoint — 10 consecutive failures auto-disables the endpoint

## Quick Start (API-only, no browser required)

1. Create an account:
   curl -X POST https://api.emithq.com/api/v1/signup \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"email": "you@example.com", "password": "your-password", "orgName": "Your Company"}'
   # Returns: { data: { orgId, apiKey, userId, tier, eventLimit, credential_storage_hint } }
   # Rate limit: 3 signups per IP per 24 hours

2. Create an application:
   curl -X POST https://api.emithq.com/api/v1/app \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer emhq_YOUR_API_KEY" \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"name": "my-app"}'

3. Create an endpoint (where webhooks will be delivered):
   curl -X POST https://api.emithq.com/api/v1/app/APP_ID/endpoint \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer emhq_YOUR_API_KEY" \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"url": "https://your-server.com/webhooks"}'
   # Returns signing secret (whsec_...) — save it for verification

4. Send a webhook event:
   curl -X POST https://api.emithq.com/api/v1/app/APP_ID/msg \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer emhq_YOUR_API_KEY" \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"eventType": "invoice.paid", "payload": {"amount": 9900}}'

5. Check delivery status:
   curl https://api.emithq.com/api/v1/app/APP_ID/msg/MSG_ID \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer emhq_YOUR_API_KEY"

## Authentication

All API requests use Bearer token authentication:
  Authorization: Bearer emhq_YOUR_API_KEY

API keys are created during signup (POST /api/v1/signup) or via the API key management endpoints.
Keys use the `emhq_` prefix. Store securely — plaintext is shown once at creation.

## API Reference

Base URL: https://api.emithq.com
OpenAPI Spec: https://emithq.com/openapi.json

### Signup
POST /api/v1/signup — Create account, org, and API key in one request. No browser required.
  Body: { "email": "...", "password": "...", "orgName": "..." }
  Returns: { data: { orgId, apiKey, userId, tier, eventLimit, credential_storage_hint } }
  Rate limit: 3/IP/day. Disposable emails blocked.

### Applications
POST /api/v1/app — Create application
GET  /api/v1/app — List applications
GET  /api/v1/app/:appId — Get application

### Endpoints
POST   /api/v1/app/:appId/endpoint — Create endpoint
GET    /api/v1/app/:appId/endpoint — List endpoints
GET    /api/v1/app/:appId/endpoint/:epId — Get endpoint
PUT    /api/v1/app/:appId/endpoint/:epId — Update endpoint
DELETE /api/v1/app/:appId/endpoint/:epId — Soft-delete endpoint
POST   /api/v1/app/:appId/endpoint/:epId/test — Send test webhook

### Messages
POST /api/v1/app/:appId/msg — Send message (triggers delivery to all matching endpoints)
GET  /api/v1/app/:appId/msg — List messages
GET  /api/v1/app/:appId/msg/:msgId — Get message + delivery attempts

### Replay
POST /api/v1/app/:appId/msg/:msgId/replay — Replay a failed message to all or specific endpoints
  Body: { "endpointIds": ["ep-1"] } (optional — omit to replay to all)

### API Key Management
POST   /api/v1/auth/keys — Create a new API key
GET    /api/v1/auth/keys — List active keys (metadata only)
POST   /api/v1/auth/keys/:keyId/rotate — Rotate key with grace period (default: 60 min)
DELETE /api/v1/auth/keys/:keyId — Revoke key immediately

### Billing
GET  /api/v1/billing/subscription — Current plan and usage
POST /api/v1/billing/checkout — Create Stripe checkout session
POST /api/v1/billing/portal — Stripe customer portal URL

### Transform Preview
POST /api/v1/transform/preview — Test payload transformations without persisting
  Body: { "payload": {...}, "rules": [...] }

## Error Codes

All errors: { error: { code: string, message: string } }

| Code | HTTP | Meaning |
|------|------|---------|
| validation_error | 400 | Invalid or missing request fields |
| unauthorized | 401 | Missing or invalid API key |
| forbidden | 403 | Feature requires upgrade (e.g., transforms on free tier) |
| not_found | 404 | Resource does not exist |
| conflict | 409 | Resource already exists |
| rate_limited | 429 | Too many requests |
| quota_exceeded | 429 | Monthly event limit reached |
| signup_failed | 500 | Account creation failed |

## Retry Strategy

Failed deliveries retry with exponential backoff + full jitter:
  Attempt 1: immediate
  Attempt 2: ~30s
  Attempt 3: ~2m
  Attempt 4: ~15m
  Attempt 5: ~1h
  Attempt 6: ~4h
  Attempt 7: ~12h
  Attempt 8: ~12h

Algorithm: delay = random(0, min(cap, initialDelay × 2^retry))
Non-retriable status codes: 400, 401, 403, 404, 410
After all retries exhausted: moved to dead-letter queue

## Webhook Signing (Standard Webhooks)

Outbound webhooks are signed per the Standard Webhooks spec:
  Headers: webhook-id, webhook-timestamp, webhook-signature
  Signed content: {webhook-id}.{webhook-timestamp}.{raw_body}
  Algorithm: HMAC-SHA256, base64-encoded
  Secret format: whsec_{base64_random}

Verification: use crypto.timingSafeEqual — never string equality.

## Quota Headers

Every authenticated response includes:
  X-EmitHQ-Quota-Limit: 100000
  X-EmitHQ-Quota-Used: 50000
  X-EmitHQ-Quota-Remaining: 50000
  X-EmitHQ-Quota-Reset: 2026-04-01T00:00:00.000Z
  X-EmitHQ-Tier: free

At 80% usage: X-EmitHQ-Quota-Warning: approaching_limit
At 95% usage: X-EmitHQ-Quota-Warning: critical_limit

## Pricing

| Tier | Events/mo | Price | Endpoints | Retention | Retries |
|------|-----------|-------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| Free | 100,000 | $0 | 10 | 3 days | 5 (fixed) |
| Starter | 500,000 | $49/mo | 50 | 14 days | 10 (configurable) |
| Growth | 2,000,000 | $149/mo | 250 | 30 days | 20 (configurable) |
| Scale | 10,000,000 | $349/mo | Unlimited | 90 days | 50 (configurable) |

Payload transformation available on Starter+ tiers.
Free tier hard-blocks at quota. Paid tiers allow overage (metered by Stripe).

## TypeScript SDK

npm install @emithq/sdk

```typescript
import { EmitHQ } from '@emithq/sdk';

const emithq = new EmitHQ('emhq_your_api_key');

// Send a webhook event
await emithq.sendEvent('app_id', {
  eventType: 'invoice.paid',
  payload: { amount: 9900, currency: 'usd' },
});

// Create an endpoint
const endpoint = await emithq.createEndpoint('app_id', {
  url: 'https://example.com/webhooks',
});

// Verify inbound webhook signature
import { verifyWebhook } from '@emithq/sdk';
const isValid = await verifyWebhook(rawBody, headers, 'whsec_your_secret');
```

SDK features: zero dependencies, fetch-based, typed errors, auto-retry on 5xx/408/429, WebCrypto verification (works in Node, browsers, edge runtimes).

## Comparison with Alternatives

### vs Svix ($0 / $490+ / Custom)
- EmitHQ: $49/mo for 500K events. Svix: $490/mo minimum paid tier.
- EmitHQ: JSONPath + template transforms (no-code). Svix: JavaScript code editor (Pro+ only).
- Both use Standard Webhooks spec for signing.
- EmitHQ: AGPL-3.0. Svix: MIT (limited).

### vs Hookdeck ($0 / $39 / $499+ / Custom)
- EmitHQ: Inbound + outbound in one platform. Hookdeck: Inbound-focused (Outpost for outbound, separate).
- EmitHQ: 50 evt/s default throughput (Starter). Hookdeck: 5 evt/s (all tiers), throughput scaling is a paid add-on.
- EmitHQ: Standard Webhooks signing. Hookdeck: Custom headers (x-hookdeck-*).

### vs Building Your Own
- Webhook delivery requires: retry queue, exponential backoff, signing, circuit breakers, dead letter queue, delivery dashboard, rate limiting, tenant isolation
- Estimated build time: 8-12 weeks of senior engineering
- EmitHQ provides all of this from $49/mo
